name: Chocolate Purses with Rhubarb-Raspberry Mousse
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cup Fresh or frozen rhubarb, finely diced (about 1/2 lb)
Confectioner's sugar for kneading and rolling
recipeInstructions:
1. Place a medium mixing bowl and the beaters of your electric mixer in the refrigerator to chill for at least 30 minutes.
2. MAKE THE MOUSSE:
a. Combine rhubarb and sugar in a medium saucepan. Cover and bring to a simmer over low heat.
b. Cook for 8-10 minutes or until rhubarb is very soft. Transfer to the bowl of a food processor and puree.
c. Transfer puree to a medium nonreactive mixing bowl and set aside.
3. Put 2 tablespoons cold water in a small saucepan and sprinkle gelatin over. Set aside and let soften for 5 minutes.
b. Heat mixture over low heat, swirling until gelatin is completely dissolved.
c. Stir gelatin into pureed rhubarb and allow to cool.
d. Fold sour cream and berries into the puree.
e. In chilled bowl, whip cream with an electric mixer at high speed until holds soft peaks. Do not overbeat.
f. Gently fold whipped cream into fruit mixture using a rubber spatula.
g. Cover mousse and refrigerate until sets, about 4 hours or overnight.
4. MAKE THE SAUCE:
a. Combine raspberries and confectioner's sugar in a small saucepan.
b. Cook over low heat until berries are soft, about 3-4 minutes.
c. Transfer to a food processor or blender and puree. Strain through fine-mesh strainer into a bowl and discard seeds.
d. Stir 2 tablespoons cold water into the sauce to thin. Transfer to a jar and refrigerate.
5. PREPARE THE MOLDING CHOCOLATE:
a. Put both chocolates in the top of a small double boiler over simmering water.
b. Melt over low heat, stirring constantly with spatula or wooden spoon until chocolate is smooth and shiny.
c. Remove from heat and add corn syrup and liquor.
d. Mix vigorously using a wooden spoon (chocolate may seize; don't worry).
e. Divide chocolate into 2 equal portions.
f. Place one portion on plastic wrap, level with spatula, cover with second sheet of plastic wrap the same size as first.
g. Roll gently to form rectangle about 10 by 15 inches and 1/16 inch thick.
h. Remove plastic wrap; dust work surface generously with confectioner's sugar.
i. Gather chocolate into a ball. If too dry, knead briefly until soft and no longer sticky.
j. Divide into 4 balls and shape each one.
6. ASSEMBLE THE PURSES:
a. Brush off any chocolate crumbs on the work surface; it should be dry.
b. Dust work surface and rolling pin with confectioner's sugar.
c. Flatten one chocolate ball slightly and roll gently into circle about 9 inches in diameter, lifting occasionally to dust surface and prevent sticking.
d. Place quarter of mousse in center of chocolate circle. Gather sides up to enclose filling. Pinch the purse at neck to seal mousse allowing top edges to fan out.
e. Transfer purse to a large platter, loosely cover with plastic wrap, and refrigerate.
7. Repeat rolling and filling remaining balls to make 3 more purses. Cover and refrigerate until ready to serve.
8. To Serve:
Place chocolate purse on each chilled dessert plate and spoon raspberry sauce around it.
